title: Modal verbs and semi-modals
intro: Modal verbs express modality: possibility, necessity, permission, ability, obligation, advice. They have no -s in the third person and are followed by the base form. Semi-modals behave like ordinary verbs (may take -s, or to + infinitive).

modals.example: I can swim. You can leave early.
I could run fast. Could you help me? It could rain.
It may rain. May I sit here?
You must wear a seatbelt. She must be at home.
I will call you. Will you help me?
Would you pass the salt? I would go if I could.

semi_modals.example: I have to go now. She has to wear a uniform.
You need to apply by Friday.

semi_modals.meaning: ability (when can not possible, e.g. future)
semi_modals.example: She will be able to come.
